titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/282

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=262 status=live modified=2017-03-30 13:23:37.653482+00 | translation moa=263 status=live modified=2017-03-30 13:23:39.733748+00

p.cl: Vniuersis Sancte matris Ecclesie filiis . Comes . Dunecanus de Carric . Salutem .

Nouerit vniuersitas uestra me
diuine Pietatis Intuitu dedisse ⁊ concessisse ⁊ hac mea carta confirmasse Deo ⁊ ecclesie beate Marie de
Nortberwic ⁊ sanctimonialibus ibidem deo seruientibus ⁊ seruituris inperpetuum Ecclesiam de Maybothel
cum terris eiusdem ecclesie ⁊ cum Decimis et oblacionibus ⁊ omnibus rectitudinibus ⁊ Iustis pertinenciis suis
tenendam De me ⁊ heredibus meis in puram ⁊ perpetuam elemosinam adeo Libere et quiete plenarie
et honorifice sicut aliquis comes uel Baro in Regno Scocie potest dare aliquam ecclesiam abbacie Liberius
quiecius plenius ⁊ honorifiencius .

Hiis Testibus Hectore ⁊ Alano militibus Et Gillascop senescallo de
carric . Iohanne Decano  Magistro Hugone Sumerleht . Nigillo macgilwyni . Dauid Sibaldo Magistro Alano
Alexandro ⁊ Alano filiis comitis . Et multis aliis .

div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, Earl Duncan of Carrick sends Greeting: You all should know that, moved by divine goodness, I have given, and granted, and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of Blessed Mary in North Berwick, and in perpetuity to the nuns serving God there now and in the future, the church of Maybole, with the lands of the same church, and with the teinds and oblations and all revenues rightly belionging to it, to be held of me and my heirs in pure and perpetual alms, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably, as any earl or baron in the Kingdom of Scotland is able to give any church to an abbey freely, quietly, fully and honourably. With these witness, Hector and Alan, knights, and Gillespie the steward of Carrick, John the dean, Master Hugh, Somerled, Niall mac Gilla Winnin, David Sibbald, Master Alan, Alexander and Alan the earl’s sons, and many others.
